nodes.label: AI as tutor for learning and understanding
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or hallucinating information source
nodes.grounding_span: I tailored my AI to respond to me in a certain way so that I could be prepared for my shelf exams and for questions on the job

nodes.label: AI as clinical information source
nodes.label_negative: Relying solely on memory without AI
nodes.grounding_span: if I get pimped on a subject that I'm pretty weak in, my attending tells me to look it up

nodes.label: AI as clinical information source
nodes.label_negative: AI errors in calculations and clinical reasoning
nodes.grounding_span: I used AI to help me narrow down diagnoses and help me come up with firstline treatment plans for my SOAP note

nodes.label: AI for iterative SOAP note refinement
nodes.label_negative: AI-generated SOAP note without attending-specific formatting
nodes.grounding_span: I go back and forth with the process. For example, if it doesn't include the necessary information for past medical history or social history, then I'll change it

nodes.label: AI as enhanced search engine for relevant articles
nodes.grounding_span: I used open evidence AI to find me research articles for that

nodes.label: AI for creating study materials (Anki cards, summaries)
nodes.label_negative: AI hallucination and broken promises in generating materials
nodes.grounding_span: I was using AI to help me make anki cards and pdfs of summaries/notes for the pathophysiology of high yield information

nodes.label: AI as efficiency booster in healthcare
nodes.label_negative: AI replacing human emotional aspect of patient care
nodes.grounding_span: I think it helps health practitioners work more efficiently

nodes.label: AI as clinical information source
nodes.grounding_span: it'll help with not missing important information - especially since we had a problem where my attending was super upset that a radiologist had missed an important feature

nodes.label: Clinical competence and impressing superiors
nodes.grounding_span: my attendings and residents get very impressed

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: I think it helps health practitioners work more efficiently

nodes.label: Patient safety and quality of care
nodes.grounding_span: it'll help with not missing important information

nodes.label: Personalized learning and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: the explanations are geared to help me understand in a less complicated way

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for outperforming peers
nodes.grounding_span: I felt satisfied when I was able to come up with the right answer with the help of AI whereas my peers could not

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: AI got it wrong, and I was embarrassed

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: It felt like it just hallucinated and gave me the wrong links. It made me pretty upset that it kept lying to me

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as partner in patient assessment
nodes.grounding_span: I think it'd progress into working more efficiently and becoming a partner in the assessment of a patient

nodes.label: Iterative refinement and personalization
nodes.grounding_span: I tailored my AI to respond to me in a certain way

nodes.label: Experience-based boundary setting
nodes.grounding_span: I do my calculations on my own. AI messes up some things when I ask for calculations
